assertions.description: navigator.rtc exists — the RTC interface binds to Navigator inside a secure context with the RTCDiagnosticLogging runtime feature on.
navigator.rtc.startDiagnosticLogging is a function — begins a session and resolves with the session UUID.
navigator.rtc.finishDiagnosticLogging is a function — finalises the local log file.
navigator.rtc.cancelDiagnosticLogging is a function — discards the captured data (the spec's IDL name, not the explainer example's cancelLogging).
The interface is [SecureContext]: on a non-secure context navigator.rtc must be absent (on a secure context either state is spec-legal, since the runtime feature also gates it).

When bound, startDiagnosticLogging() with 6 metadata entries rejects before any recording starts (spec mandates TypeError; Chromium main currently rejects with RangeError — rtc.cc RejectWithRangeError — so either name proves enforcement).
assertions.test: (async()=>{ if (typeof navigator.rtc?.startDiagnosticLogging !== 'function') return false; const m = {}; for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) m['k' + i] = 'v'; try { await navigator.rtc.startDiagnosticLogging({ metadata: m }); return false; } catch (e) { return !!e && (e.name === 'TypeError' || e.name === 'RangeError'); } })()

assertions.description: When bound, startDiagnosticLogging() with a metadata value over 100 characters rejects before any recording starts (spec mandates TypeError; Chromium main currently rejects with RangeError, measuring UTF-8 bytes — rtc_logging_utils.h — so either name proves enforcement).
assertions.test: (async()=>{ if (typeof navigator.rtc?.startDiagnosticLogging !== 'function') return false; try { await navigator.rtc.startDiagnosticLogging({ metadata: { app: 'x'.repeat(101) } }); return false; } catch (e) { return !!e && (e.name === 'TypeError' || e.name === 'RangeError'); } })()
